steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The farm-size transition hypothesis predicts that modernization leads to consolidation into larger farms. The question asks which choice shows that Promkhambut et al.'s findings about Thailand weaken that prediction.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Although the farm-size transition hypothesis may be applicable to some countries in Asia, it is inconsistent with the development of rice farming in Thailand.: Doesn't clearly state what the hypothesis predicts or show it failed in Thailand. ✗
  • Choice B — Taken together, the studies by Masters et al. and Promkhambut et al. suggest that rice farms in Thailand have responded to the economic pressures associated with modernization by expanding in size.: Misrepresents the source — Promkhambut et al. found farms stayed small, not that they grew. ✗
  • Choice C — Masters et al. report that the average farm size "already has or will soon begin to rise" in Asia, a finding that is consistent with the farm-size transition hypothesis.: Doesn't engage with the Thai evidence that would challenge the hypothesis. ✗
  • Choice D — The predicted shift to large-scale commercial farming may not hold true for rice farms in Thailand, where, according to Promkhambut et al., rice farms have remained small as they've modernized.: States the hypothesis's prediction (shift to large-scale farming) and shows it didn't happen — Thai farms adopted modern methods while remaining small, per Promkhambut et al. ✓
That matches Choice D — The predicted shift to large-scale commercial farming may not hold true for rice farms in Thailand, where, according to Promkhambut et al., rice farms have remained small as they've modernized..
